title: Welders, Cutters, Solderers, and Brazers
description: Use hand-welding, flame-cutting, hand-soldering, or brazing equipment to weld or join metal components or to fill holes, indentations, or seams of fabricated metal products.

tasks.text: Operate safety equipment and use safe work habits.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine workpieces for defects and measure workpieces with straightedges or templates to ensure conformance with specifications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Weld components in flat, vertical, or overhead positions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Detect faulty operation of equipment or defective materials and notify supervisors.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Recognize, set up, and operate hand and power tools common to the welding trade, such as shielded metal arc and gas metal arc welding equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select and install torches, torch tips, filler rods, and flux, according to welding chart specifications or types and thicknesses of metals.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mark or tag material with proper job number, piece marks, and other identifying marks as required.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Determine required equipment and welding methods, applying knowledge of metallurgy, geometry, and welding techniques.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare all material surfaces to be welded, ensuring that there is no loose or thick scale, slag, rust, moisture, grease, or other foreign matter.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Align and clamp workpieces together, using rules, squares, or hand tools, or position items in fixtures, jigs, or vises.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Connect and turn regulator valves to activate and adjust gas flow and pressure so that desired flames are obtained.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Position and secure workpieces, using hoists, cranes, wire, and banding machines or hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Melt and apply solder along adjoining edges of workpieces to solder joints, using soldering irons, gas torches, or electric-ultrasonic equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor the fitting, burning, and welding processes to avoid overheating of parts or warping, shrinking, distortion, or expansion of material.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Grind, cut, buff, or bend edges of workpieces to be joined to ensure snug fit, using power grinders and hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Weld separately or in combination, using aluminum, stainless steel, cast iron, and other alloys.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Chip or grind off excess weld, slag, or spatter, using hand scrapers or power chippers, portable grinders, or arc-cutting equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop templates and models for welding projects, using mathematical calculations based on blueprint information.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Repair products by dismantling, straightening, reshaping, and reassembling parts, using cutting torches, straightening presses, and hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean or degrease parts, using wire brushes, portable grinders, or chemical baths.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Hammer out bulges or bends in metal workpieces.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Check grooves, angles, or gap allowances, using micrometers, calipers, and precision measuring instruments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Melt and apply solder to fill holes, indentations, or seams of fabricated metal products, using soldering equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Ignite torches or start power supplies and strike arcs by touching electrodes to metals being welded, completing electrical circuits.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Guide and direct flames or electrodes on or across workpieces to straighten, bend, melt, or build up metal.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Use fire suppression methods in industrial emergencies.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Preheat workpieces prior to welding or bending, using torches or heating furnaces.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set up and use ladders and scaffolding as necessary to complete work.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate metal shaping, straightening, and bending machines, such as brakes and shears.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Analyze engineering drawings, blueprints, specifications, sketches, work orders, and material safety data sheets to plan layout, assembly, and operations.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
